issues.summary: Fix problem with lack of communication in rtb-delivery change detection to rtb-trader (when error is detected)
issues.description:
When change detection in rtb-delivery encounters a structural error (e.g. VAST no longer contains a Full HD mediafile), no notification is sent to rtb-trader. The error is only logged and counted in Prometheus - the team has no visibility that a VAST has broken, and the creative continues serving stale video silently.

# Solution:  
  
SaxyVast Change Detection Results and Their Handling in Delivery

### Success results (list) — routed to `handle_mediafile_change_detection`:

| SaxyVast result | When | Delivery clause | What delivery does | Slack? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `[hash, etag]`  | Full HD mediafile found + server returned ETag  | `[MediafilesTagHash, MediaFileEtag]`  | `maybe_detect_mediafile_change` with ETag → compares both  | Only on change  |
| `[hash, :no_etag]`  | Full HD mediafile found, server **did not** return ETag  | `[MediafilesTagHash, no_etag]`  | counter `no_etag_in_headers` + `maybe_detect_mediafile_change` with `<<>>` → hash-only comparison  | Only on change  |
| `[]`  | `change_detection_locked: true` in opts  | `[]`  | counter `check_skipped`, nothing else  | NO  |

### Error results (tuple) — all routed to the same clause `{error, ErrorReason, ErrorDetails}`:

| SaxyVast result | When | What delivery does | Slack? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `{:error, :no_suitable_mediafile, "..."}`  | No 1920×1080 or 1080×1920 mediafile  | LOG_ERROR + counter `error` — **nothing else**  | NO (currently)  |
| `{:error, :no_mediafiles_tag, "..."}`  | Missing `<MediaFiles>` tag in XML  | LOG_ERROR + counter `error` — **nothing else**  | NO (currently)  |
| `{:error, :http_error, "HTTP error: 404"}`  | Mediafile server returned HTTP error  | LOG_ERROR + counter `error` — **nothing else**  | NO  |
| `{:error, :request_error, "..."}`  | Timeout/network issue on HEAD request  | LOG_ERROR + counter `error` — **nothing else**  | NO  |
| `{:error, :unknown, "..."}`  | Unexpected parsing error  | LOG_ERROR + counter `error` — **nothing else**  | NO  |

issues.summary: Run test campaign on TV in the office
issues.description:
### Task Description

**As a** Samsung Ads Developer, **I want** to run a test campaign for polls & surveys interactive advertisements on Samsung TV Plus **so that** I can validate the proper functionality and user experience of interactive advertising features before production deployment.

### Business Context

Interactive advertising represents a significant opportunity for Samsung Ads to increase user engagement and provide advertisers with more valuable, data-rich campaign options. Polls & surveys advertisements enable viewers to actively participate with content, creating higher engagement rates and providing actionable insights for advertisers. This test campaign is essential to ensure that the interactive advertising experience meets quality standards and delivers the expected user experience across Samsung TV Plus platforms. Proper validation of advertisement display, interactive functionality, and design compliance ensures that when this feature launches to advertisers, it will perform reliably and maintain Samsung's reputation for quality advertising solutions.

### Acceptance Criteria

* Test campaign is successfully running on SRPOL available TV devices
* Advertisement display is confirmed to render properly across test devices
* Interactive layer functionality is tested and working as expected
* Displayed advertisement compared with Figma design specifications
* Reporting data gathering is confirmed to be collecting interaction metrics
* Fallback version of the advertisement is validated (if applicable)
* All found misalignments are tracked as separate Jira issues to work on in the future

issues.summary: [rtb-delivery, adgear-data, log-converter] P&S reporting: Adjust implementation to use top-level property instead of `misc`.
issues.description:
Objective: Modify three systems to change how `iadpoll_answer_index` is stored and processed in delivery logs.

### Acceptance Criteria

* `rtb-delivery` - `iadpoll_answer_index` moved from `misc` to top-level property in Avro payload.
* `adgear-data` - Support for new `iadpoll_answer_index` property.
* `log-converter` - `adgear-data` version bump.

issues.summary: Model, database, and creative type registration for separate Poll & Survey
issues.description:
#### Background

Before any other work can start, the new creative type needs to exist in the system. This means creating the database column that controls access, building the backend model with all its rules, and registering the type so the rest of the application recognizes it. The most sensitive change here is updating the poll item database association — currently it is locked to IVA, and it needs to be opened up so poll items can belong to either IVA or the new type.

#### What to do

* Add a new feature flag column to the buyers table (`interactive_poll_ad_enabled`, default false)
* Create the new `InteractivePollAd` backend model with:

    * Validations: poll answer count (2–4), logo dimensions (296×120px), VAST URL format
    * Lifecycle hooks: trigger Remotion re-render when relevant fields change
    * Always uses the `PollSurvey` Remotion composition and the poll-survey iframe resource
    
* Register the new creative type in the type registry, seed data, and translation files
* Update the poll item association so poll items can belong to either IVA or the new type (no database schema change required)
* Add automated tests covering all validations and the save flow

#### Acceptance Criteria

* The new creative type can be created and saved with valid data
* Validation errors are returned for invalid poll configurations (wrong answer count, bad logo dimensions, invalid VAST URL)
* Existing IVA poll creatives are completely unaffected — all existing IVA tests remain green

### Background

`vertica-rollups` is a Spark job that reads processed Avro delivery logs from S3, aggregates them into rollup records and loads the results into Vertica fact tables. The rollup record schema is code-generated from `table.sql`.

A single `iadpoll` event carries the answer index in the `misc` map field as `{"iadpoll_answer_index": "N"}`. The event logic must read this value from `misc` and use it to increment both the general `iadpoll_answers` counter and the specific `iadpoll_answer_N` counter simultaneously — this is the same multi-column increment pattern used by `impression` events.

> **Note:** This is a non-standard approach compared to other metrics which read dedicated typed fields. The `misc` map must be parsed at runtime and the key `"iadpoll_answer_index"` looked up. A missing or malformed value should be handled gracefully (increment only `iadpoll_answers`, skip per-answer counters).

### Description

Add 5 new metric columns to the rollup schema, implement the aggregation logic in the event logic class (reading the answer index from `misc`) and update the SQL rollup script to insert the new columns into `f_buyer_delivery_all`.

### Acceptance Criteria

* Five new metric columns (`iadpoll_answers`, `iadpoll_answer_1` through `iadpoll_answer_4`) are present in the rollup schema and generated POJO
* A non-dropped `iadpoll` event correctly increments `iadpoll_answers` and the appropriate per-answer counter based on the value in `misc["iadpoll_answer_index"]`
* A missing or invalid `iadpoll_answer_index` in `misc` increments only `iadpoll_answers` without error
* The rollup SQL correctly aggregates and inserts all 5 new columns into `f_buyer_delivery_all`
* A new release of `vertica-rollups` is cut
* Airflow configuration is updated with the new jar version

### Tech Scope of Work

`core/src/main/resources/trader/delivery/table.sql`  
Add 5 new metric columns (`iadpoll_answers`, `iadpoll_answer_1` through `iadpoll_answer_4`) with `INT NOT NULL
